title: Jurassic Park computers in excruciating detail
titleZh: 巨细无遗解析《侏罗纪公园》中的电脑
author: vinhnx
relevanceReason: 文章详细分析了电影《侏罗纪公园》中出现的各种计算机硬件和软件，包括 Apple Powerbook 100 等经典设备的处理器、内存、屏幕分辨率和操作系统等技术细节，属于科技硬件和计算机历史相关内容。
summaryRaw: Jul 13, 2026 Jurassic Park computers in excruciating detail
After I mentioned a Jurassic Park anecdote the other day, I watched the movie again. I must have seen it at least ten times now. This time, I researched every computer/software I spotted.
EDIT: Just when I was putting the final touches on this article, I read the sad news that Sam Neill, who played paleontologist Alan Grant in JP, has passed away today. R.I.P Sam.

Apple Powerbook 100 Surprisingly, the first computer visible is not on the island Isla Nublar but in Alan Grant and Ellie Sattler's mobile trailer. It is an Apple Powerbook 100, visible in the image below on the left side. Apple Powerbook 100

It had a Motorola 68000 processor at 16 MHz, 2–8 megabytes (MB) of RAM, a 9-inch (23 cm) monochrome backlit liquid-crystal display (LCD) with 640 × 400 pixel resolution, and the System 7.0.1 operating system. Wikipedia
summaryZh: 作者详细研究了电影《侏罗纪公园》中出现的所有计算机和软件设备
文章分析了 Apple Powerbook 100 等经典硬件的技术规格，包括 Motorola 68000 处理器、System 7.0.1 操作系统等
文章发布时恰逢饰演 Alan Grant 的演员 Sam Neill 去世
highlightsZh: 深入挖掘经典电影中的科技道具细节
展示 90 年代早期移动计算设备的硬件规格
结合电影场景与真实技术历史的独特视角
引发科技爱好者对经典硬件的怀旧讨论

comments.author: kalleboo
comments.textRawHtml:
> It is unclear how Jurassic Park crew got their hands on a Motorola Envoy

The head of frogdesign (Hartmut Esslinger) ended up running into Spielberg on a plane and showed it to him. The one in the movie is an original mockup.

Source: https://www.therpf.com/forums/threads/jurassic-park-tablet-d...

Discussion: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=46752261
comments.textZhHtml:
> 目前尚不清楚侏罗纪公园的工作人员是如何获得摩托罗拉特使的

frogdesign 的负责人 (Hartmut Esslinger) 最终在飞机上遇到了斯皮尔伯格并向他展示了它。电影中的是原始模型。

来源：https://www.therpf.com/forums/threads/jurassic-park-tablet-d...

讨论：https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=46752261

children.author: ronbenton
children.textRawHtml: IMO this is the social internet at its best. Pretty obscure question answered relatively quickly with answer and source.
children.textZhHtml: 我认为这是最好的社交互联网。相当晦涩的问题相对较快地得到了答案和来源。

children.author: Waterluvian
children.textRawHtml:
AI immediately gives me the same answer. I can’t tell if I like this easy access to detail or lament the growing irrelevance of “social internet” for these kinds of things.

It reminds me of pre-phone disagreements among pals. You’d argue and argue and maybe eventually agree to disagree. Today someone just looks up the trivia and it’s all over.
children.textZhHtml:
人工智能立即给了我同样的答案。我不知道我是否喜欢这种轻松获取细节的方式，还是感叹“社交互联网”对此类事情越来越不相关。

这让我想起了朋友之间电话前的分歧。你们会争论再争论，也许最终会同意不同意。今天有人随便查一下琐事就完事了。

children.author: pimlottc
children.textRawHtml: Of course, the only reason AI knows about it is because one human posted it online because they wanted to share a good story with another human
children.textZhHtml: 当然，人工智能知道它的唯一原因是一个人将其发布到网上，因为他们想与另一个人分享一个好故事

children.author: justusthane
children.textRawHtml: I really worry about AI leading to the information death of the internet. If everyone is getting their info from AI, rather than actually reading websites/blogs/forums, is there any incentive for posting things like this on the internet in the first place?
children.textZhHtml: 我真的很担心人工智能会导致互联网的信息死亡。如果每个人都从人工智能获取信息，而不是实际阅读网站/博客/论坛，那么首先是否有动力在互联网上发布此类内容？

children.author: bdcravens
children.textRawHtml: I don't know if I'm unique in this, but I find myself asking people I know about things that I know I can easily lookup faster. At this point it's more a social ritual than actual information gathering.
children.textZhHtml: 我不知道我在这方面是否独一无二，但我发现自己向我认识的人询问我知道我可以轻松更快地查找的事情。在这一点上，这更像是一种社交仪式，而不是实际的信息收集。

children.author: order-matters
children.textRawHtml:
same here, but only when it feels like good conversation or is a subject we already discuss. Especially if youre in the same room, then it can become a little trivia game and if no one knows then someone can look it up

related note, my girlfriend is bilingual with spanish, and i only have some old high school classes of spanish to go off of, so whenever she texts me a word i dont recognize i ask her what it means. Aside from helping me understand, she gets a peek into my literacy level (which is admittedly pretty low), i can call out the word when its used again, and i get the impression she likes teaching me these little things.

extending the lesson beyond our little ritual, when you ask another person for the information it goes beyond being useful to each other. it is a bid for connection, and a display that you trust them and their opinion/knowledge on the subject.
children.textZhHtml:
这里也一样，但前提是感觉像是很好的对话或者是我们已经讨论过的主题。 特别是如果你们在同一个房间，那么它可能会变成一个小问答游戏，如果没有人知道，那么有人可以查一下

相关注释，我的女朋友会说西班牙语，而我只有一些旧的高中西班牙语课要上，所以每当她给我发短信时，我不认识一个单词，我会问她这意味着什么。 除了帮助我理解之外，她还了解了我的识字水平（诚然，我的识字水平相当低），当我再次使用这个词时，我可以喊出这个词，而且我觉得她喜欢教我这些小事情。

将课程扩展到我们的小仪式之外，当你向另一个人询问信息时，它不仅仅是对彼此有用。 这是一种建立联系的方式，并表明您信任他们以及他们对该主题的看法/知识。

children.author: seedboot
children.textRawHtml: It's just like we evolved to be social creatures!
children.textZhHtml: 这就像我们进化成为社交生物一样！

children.author: MisterTea
children.textRawHtml:
> AI immediately gives me the same answer.

"The search engine found the answer too" is not an interesting response.
children.textZhHtml:
> AI 立即给了我同样的答案。

“搜索引擎也找到了答案”并不是一个有趣的回答。

children.author: dylan604
children.textRawHtml: In the early days of the smart phone, I had heard it referred to as the Bar Bet Settler 5000. It was pulled out of one's pocket and with its web browser one would use Google's search page to find information to settle the bet. Then, those smart phones got infected with social media apps and the Bar Bet Settler 5000 went the way of the dodo.
children.textZhHtml: 在智能手机的早期，我听说它被称为 Bar Bet Settler 5000。它是从口袋里拿出来的，通过它的网络浏览器，人们可以使用 Google 的搜索页面来查找信息来结算赌注。然后，这些智能手机感染了社交媒体应用程序，Bar Bet Settler 5000 也步了渡渡鸟的后尘。

children.author: Forgeties79
children.textRawHtml: As more people offload their search/mental effort to LLM’s and fewer people take the time to answer these obscure questions, unfortunately we will simply lose the fun portion while making LLM’s incapable of answering them. So good news is you don’t have to make a decision!
children.textZhHtml: 随着越来越多的人将他们的搜索/脑力工作转移给法学硕士，而花时间回答这些晦涩的问题的人越来越少，不幸的是，我们只会失去其中的乐趣，同时使法学硕士无法回答这些问题。好消息是您不必做出决定！

children.author: fabiensanglard
children.textRawHtml: Thanks, I am going to update the article!

children.author: vishnugupta
children.textRawHtml: Thank you! What a beautiful and appropriate meta comment!
children.textZhHtml: 谢谢！多么美丽而恰当的元评论！

comments.author: kalleboo
comments.textRawHtml:
> Some code associated with Nedryland is visible on screen. It looks like actual source code[9] with Classic Mac OS API functions calls

The source code shown is example code included with the Macintosh Programmers Workshop, Apple's original IDE for the Mac. Originally sold as a separate product, eventually it was provided on the Developer CDs and then as a free online download as serious developers had moved to CodeWarrior. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macintosh_Programmer's_Worksho...

One of the windows shows the example for how to make a HyperCard XCMD and the other one looks like an MPW script for using Apple's Projector source control.

edit: Found the files in question in a copy of MPW 3.1. Line endings have been converted from CR to LF and the character set from MacOS Roman to UTF-8 to display easily in modern browsers

MPW 3.1:Examples:HyperXExamples:Reduce.p https://kalleboo.com/linked/Reduce.p.txt

MPW 3.1:Examples:Examples:CheckOutActive https://kalleboo.com/linked/CheckOutActive.txt

MPW 3.1:Examples:Examples:DerezPict https://kalleboo.com/linked/DerezPict.txt
comments.textZhHtml:
> 一些与 Nedryland 相关的代码在屏幕上可见。它看起来像带有经典 Mac OS API 函数调用的实际源代码[9]

显示的源代码是 Macintosh Programmers Workshop（Apple 最初的 Mac IDE）中包含的示例代码。最初作为单独的产品出售，最终它以开发人员 CD 的形式提供，然后随着认真的开发人员转向 CodeWarrior，作为免费的在线下载。 https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Macintosh_Programmer's_Worksho...

其中一个窗口显示了如何制作 HyperCard XCMD 的示例，另一个窗口看起来像使用 Apple 投影仪源代码控制的 MPW 脚本。

编辑：在 MPW 3.1 的副本中找到了有问题的文件。行尾已从 CR 转换为 LF，字符集从 MacOS Roman 转换为 UTF-8，以便在现代浏览器中轻松显示

MPW 3.1:Examples:HyperXExamples:Reduce.p https://kalleboo.com/linked/Reduce.p.txt

MPW 3.1:Examples:Examples:CheckOutActive https://kalleboo.com/linked/CheckOutActive.txt

MPW 3.1：示例：示例：DerezPict https://kalleboo.com/linked/DerezPict.txt

comments.author: pivo
comments.textRawHtml:
My wife worked for Thinking Machines back then. I remember that they'd asked Cray to loan them a supercomputer for the film because that's the computer used in the book. Cray brushed them off, so they turned to Thinking Machines who were happy to do it.

To thank them, the producers rented a theater in Cambridge, MA to screen the film just for Thinking Machines and I was also able to attend. By far the biggest reactions from the audience that night were when the CM-5 was shown for the first time and then when the young actress says, "It's a Unix system. I know this"
comments.textZhHtml:
我的妻子当时在 Thinking Machines 工作。我记得他们要求克雷借给他们一台用于电影的超级计算机，因为这就是书中使用的计算机。克雷拒绝了他们，所以他们转向 Thinking Machines，他们很乐意这样做。

为了感谢他们，制片人在马萨诸塞州剑桥租了一个剧院专门为 Thinking Machines 放映这部电影，我也能参加。到目前为止，当晚观众最大的反应是当 CM-5 第一次展示时，然后当年轻女演员说：“这是一个 Unix 系统。我知道这一点”

children.author: dhosek
children.textRawHtml: “It’s a Unix system. I know this.” was definitely the line from the movie most quoted in online spaces in the 90s.
children.textZhHtml: “这是一个 Unix 系统。我知道这一点。”这绝对是 90 年代网络空间中引用次数最多的电影台词。

children.author: kraquepype
children.textRawHtml:
I wonder how many sysadmin or adjacent careers that scene helped to foster.

As a kid JP was a favorite of mine, and eventually I got into Linux as an older teen.

That sequence cemented Unix as this mythical thing in my mind and I knew I had to know more. I eventually got to work on AIX in my first sysadmin role which was great. I leaned more into Linux, but it had a positive effect on my IT career.
children.textZhHtml:
我想知道这个场景帮助培养了多少系统管理员或相关职业。

小时候 JP 是我的最爱，最终我在十几岁的时候进入了 Linux。

这个序列巩固了 Unix 在我心目中的神话般的地位，我知道我必须了解更多。我最终开始在 AIX 上工作，担任我的第一个系统管理员角色，这很棒。我更倾向于 Linux，但这对我的 IT 职业生涯产生了积极的影响。
